# projectionMatrix

**Instance Property**

A transformation matrix that determines the extent of a scene visible to the camera.

## Declaration

```swift
var projectionMatrix: matrix_float4x4 { get }
```

## Discussion

Model I/O  automatically derives this property from the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLCamera/nearVisibilityDistance], [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLCamera/farVisibilityDistance], and [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLCamera/fieldOfView] properties. A renderer uses this matrix, along with view and model matrices derived from the camera’s position and orientation (its inherited [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject/transform] property) and the content to be rendered, to transform vertex data to the renderer’s 2D screen space at render time.

## Managing Camera Perspective

- **projection**: The style of projection transform used by the camera.
- **MDLCameraProjection**: Options for camera projection styles, used by the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLCamera/projection] property.
- **nearVisibilityDistance**: The camera’s near depth limit.
- **farVisibilityDistance**: The camera’s far depth limit.
- **fieldOfView**: The camera’s field of view, in degrees.
- **ray(to:forViewPort:)**: Returns a point, in 3D world coordinates, corresponding to the specified 2D view coordinates.
- **worldToMetersConversionScale**: The scale factor to meters from the world coordinate system containing the camera.
